text: The surgeon completes the laparoscopic suturing by tying the knot. The needle is held at a 45-degree angle in the non-dominant hand, positioned north of the knot. The first wrap involves two clockwise turns with the needle to the left of the knot and the suture tag on the right. The dominant hand instrument falls on top of the tag after rotation. The non-dominant hand moves to the one o'clock position, while the dominant hand leaves the tag at seven o'clock. The second wrap involves a counterclockwise rotation, with the non-dominant hand at eleven o'clock and the dominant hand at five o'clock. The final wrap is a single clockwise turn, completing the surgeon's knot.
